policy: Port public behavior into active Kettle/Jem APIs; do not recreate old internal helper classes unless they remain the best active shape.

superseded_decisions.rationale: Old task classes exposed internal sequencing details that are now represented by active plan/apply/install reports, phase reports, and explicit install_steps.
superseded_decisions.active_contract: Task APIs remain public orchestration entry points, but callers assert report shape and generated bytes rather than old helper-object collaboration.

superseded_decisions.rationale: TTY prompt adapters are replaced by a machine-readable prompt transport and prompt-answer input contract usable by CLI, Rake, and future HTTP envelopes.
superseded_decisions.active_contract: DecisionPolicy emits prompt_requests and accepts prompt_answers, KETTLE_JEM_PROMPT_ANSWERS, and --prompt-answer ID=ACTION.

superseded_decisions.rationale: Parser-specific merge helper internals moved behind active Ruby, Markdown, YAML, TOML, ast-merge, ast-crispr, and tree_haver provider contracts.
superseded_decisions.active_contract: Kettle/Jem asserts package templating behavior locally and relies on lower-level provider fixture suites for parser-specific merge mechanics.

superseded_decisions.rationale: Named phase classes are no longer the public behavior boundary; the active system exposes stable phase reports and plugin lifecycle diagnostics.
superseded_decisions.active_contract: Phase order, per-phase stats, and plugin before/after callbacks are asserted through active Kettle/Jem report and plugin specs.

superseded_decisions.rationale: Template helper micro-APIs were implementation details around token replacement, output routing, and old source merging.
superseded_decisions.active_contract: Active specs assert token projection, recipe metadata, final generated bytes, and selftest/template manifest behavior through public Kettle/Jem APIs.

files.notes: Generated bin/setup preserves the old quiet bundle-install behavior through the packaged template and scaffold system spec.

remaining_behaviors: full old executable option parity across bootstrap, template, install, prepare, and selftest commands
byte-level self-templating convergence for the default executable happy path
files.notes: Active CLI/setup specs now cover setup command reporting, first-run config bootstrap, configured project handoff, clean/preflight checks, option projection, bundled handoff, bootstrap commit policy, and version file bootstrap.

ported_behaviors: install task no longer invokes template implicitly
README and gemspec templating behavior moved to active template specs
destination Gemfile owns setup commands in monorepos
post-template mise cleanup, README compatibility badge trim, homepage repair, and .env.local gitignore safety
template/setup binstub validation against real monorepo destination

files.notes: Active CLI/Rake specs cover include option parsing/reporting and active plan/apply recipe filtering covers only/include behavior.

ported_behaviors: interactive prompt transport and answer input contract
legacy Markdown README H1, nested subsection, and fenced-code preservation
remaining_behaviors: full workflow/appraisal matrix pruning parity against old generated workflow set
duplicate drift checks during template/install runs
README badge alignment with actual generated workflow files

ported_behaviors: machine-readable and markdown drift report shape
remaining_behaviors: destination-gem drift scoring backed by kettle-drift semantics
files.notes: Selftest now validates template output against a destination and emits machine-readable plus markdown drift reports.
Active install bootstraps version_gem version and entrypoint files after gemspec templating and before bin/setup or Bundler handoff.
Active Rake task surface registers prepare through packaged rakelib.

remaining_behaviors: Rake selftest default destination behavior against Kettle/Jem itself
files.notes: Active Rake task surface registers selftest and delegates to the shared task implementation.
Generated Rakefiles now expose the active task surface when kettle-jem is installed.
Template manifest and checksum metadata are active public APIs.
Selftest report rendering is covered through the active task and diagnostic report specs.
